---
# Local account creation (net user /add)

**Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Spot local-account creation for persistence: PROCESS command lines that `contains "user"` AND `contains "/add"` catch `net user <name> <pass> /add` and `net localgroup administrators <name> /add` regardless of the leading binary (`net.exe`, `net1.exe`, a PowerShell wrapper). A new account — especially one immediately added to Administrators — right after initial access is a classic T1136 follow-on. Pivot on `causality_actor_process_image_name` for the creator.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and actor_process_command_line contains "user" and actor_process_command_line contains "/add"
| fields _time, actor_process_image_name, actor_process_command_line, causality_actor_process_image_name
```

_Query syntax live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (narrow 30-min window). Adapt thresholds + the `contains`/`in` sets to your environment; filter early to keep the scan cheap._
